title: Inside the 3D Pipeline — How We Build Worlds for VR

slides.title: Inside the 3D Pipeline
slides.subtitle:
How We Build Worlds for VR, AR & Beyond

NDF — Game · VR · AR · Metaverse Studio

[Name A] — 3D Modeler / [Specialty]

slides.notes: Each presenter introduces themselves briefly. Keep it casual. [INSERT TEAM PHOTOS — one per person, or a group photo]

slides.title: What We Build at NDF 🏢

bullets.text: VR Training Simulations — learn real jobs in virtual worlds
AR Experiences — digital layers on the real world
Games — interactive 3D worlds
Web & App — 3D on every platform
We specialize in low-poly, real-time 3D — not film CGI, not 3D printing. Every polygon has to earn its place.

slides.notes: Quick overview. [INSERT 4 PROJECT SCREENSHOTS — one for each category: VR/AR/Game/Web]. The last bullet is the key differentiator.

slides.title: Why Are We Here Today?

bullets.text: Today we're NOT going to teach you how to model.
We're going to teach you how to survive in the real world.
Learning to model — that's one thing.
But the skills that really make your career?
Communication, teamwork, and knowing how to deliver.

slides.notes: This is the core message. Pause after the last bullet. Let it sink in.

slides.title: The Real Pipeline — It's a Relay 🔄

bullets.text: Modeler (Us) → Build the mesh, clean topology, proper naming
2D Artist → Add textures & materials (depends on our clean UV)
Animator → Rig & animate (depends on our clean topology)
If your mesh is messy → the texture artist struggles
If your pivot is wrong → the animator has to fix YOUR mistake
Your job isn't done when it looks good. It's done when the next person can use it.

slides.notes: [INSERT 3 SCREENSHOTS side by side: wireframe mesh → textured model → rigged/animated model, showing the relay handoff]

slides.title: Your Model Isn't For You 🤝

bullets.text: Clean mesh — no stray verts, no overlapping faces
Correct pivot points — a door rotates from the side, not the middle
Face direction — all normals facing outward
Proper naming — Project_Asset_v03_date
Talk to the next person — "How do you want this?"
The job is done when the next person can use it without asking you a single question
[INSERT: Maya outliner screenshot with clean naming]
[INSERT: Pivot point example — correct vs wrong]

slides.notes: This slide is about professional respect. Your model isn't for your portfolio — it's for the team.

slides.title: Name It Like a Pro 📁

bullets.text: model_final.fbx
model_final_v2.fbx
model_FINAL_REAL.fbx
model_FINAL_REAL_fixed.fbx
JobVR_EspressoMachine_v01_20260401.fbx
JobVR_EspressoMachine_v02_20260405.fbx
JobVR_EspressoMachine_v03_20260410.fbx
Your file name should tell: WHAT, WHICH version, WHEN

slides.notes: This always gets a laugh. Everyone has done the 'final_FINAL' thing. Be honest about it.

slides.title: Communication is Your Superpower 💬

bullets.text: Break down unclear requirements — "make it cool" → actual task list
Brief the team clearly — clear tasks, clear deadlines
Shield team from client chaos — you handle the changes
Speak up when there's a problem — don't hide until deadline
Propose solutions, not just complaints
Ask for help early — silence isn't strength, it's a bottleneck

slides.notes: Core message reinforcement: "Don't be the person who makes problems. Be the person who makes everyone's job easier."

slides.title: When the Client Changes Everything 🌪️

bullets.text: Client's fortune teller said the design was "unlucky" — redo everything
Client goes silent for weeks → drops 50 comments the day before deadline
"Can you add 10 more characters? The deadline is still the same."
"I want it low-poly but also... really detailed and realistic"
How you HANDLE it matters more than how you FEEL about it.

slides.notes: Tell the real stories! Fortune teller story = guaranteed laughs. End with: "You don't have to believe me right now. But when it happens to you — and it will — you'll remember this conversation." [INSERT: Meme or funny visual about client revisions]

slides.title: AI in Our Workflow — What Actually Works 🤖

bullets.text: Concept art & mood boards — fast exploration
Reference generation — try 10 styles in minutes
Idea brainstorming — "what if the robot was more friendly?"
AI helps you explore ideas FAST
But the final model? That's still us.
[INSERT: AI-generated concept art used as reference for a real project]

slides.notes: Be positive about AI here — it's genuinely useful for concept/reference stage.

slides.title: AI 3D Generation — The Pretty Lie 🎭

bullets.text: Render view: looks amazing!
Wireframe view: topology is a DISASTER
Can't rig, can't animate, can't optimize for VR
[INSERT: AI mesh render + wireframe]
Render view: maybe less flashy at first
Wireframe view: clean topology!
Ready for texture, rig, animation, VR — EVERYTHING
[INSERT: Hand model render + wireframe]

slides.notes: This is the knockout punch: AI can generate a mesh — but it can't name the file properly, set the pivot, or ask the animator what they need. AI has no manners.

slides.title: Mistakes We've All Made 😅

bullets.text: Detailing too early — blockout first!
Not talking to the next person in the pipeline
Hiding problems instead of speaking up
Naming files "final_v2_REAL_thisone.fbx"
Forgetting scale — giant coffee cup in VR
Buying assets without checking topology
[INSERT: Funny fail screenshot]
Wrong scale, broken normals, messy outliner...
We've been there. You will too.
The key: learn fast, don't repeat.

slides.notes: Keep it fun. Humor makes mistakes memorable.

slides.title: What We Hope You Remember 🧠

bullets.text: 🤝 Communicate — Talk to your team. Ask before you assume. Speak up when stuck.
🧹 Clean Handoff — Your work isn't for you. It's for the next person. Make their life easy.
🌪️ Adapt — Clients change their mind. Deadlines shift. Roll with it.
"Be the coworker everyone wants to work with."

slides.notes: Pause on the last quote. This is the takeaway they should carry with them.

slides.title: Your Turn — Ask Us Anything! 🎤

bullets.text: What software should I learn first?
Will AI replace 3D modelers?
How long to become job-ready?
How did you get into this career?
What's the hardest part of the job?
Technical questions? Go for it.
Career advice? We're open.
Personal journey? Happy to share.
[INSERT: Team photo or fun behind-the-scenes shot]

slides.notes: These starter questions are for shy audiences. Encourage ALL questions. Split answers between team members — juniors answer career/learning questions, DewS handles client/industry questions.
